title: Phrasal Verbs
description: 30 phrasal verbs essenciais para o dia a dia, com nota sobre quando são separáveis.

cards.example: Don't give up on your dreams.
I'm looking for my glasses.
Can you turn on the lights, please?
She turned off her phone during the meeting.
We ran out of milk this morning.
I can't figure out how this app works.
I wake up at six every day.
He got up late and missed the bus.
I'll pick you up at the airport.
She drops the kids off at school every morning.
I just found out that she's moving to Canada.
What's going on here?
He came back from vacation yesterday.
Can you look after my dog this weekend?

cards.back: estar ansioso por, aguardar com expectativa
cards.example: I'm looking forward to seeing you next week.
Put on your jacket; it's cold outside.

cards.back: tirar (roupa); decolar (avião) — separável no sentido de roupa
cards.example: The plane took off on time.
I get along really well with my coworkers.
We hung out at the mall after class.
He showed up an hour late to the meeting.
I need to set up my new computer.
Don't throw away those boxes; we might need them.
Can I try on these jeans?
They called off the game because of the rain.
Stop putting off your dentist appointment.
Carry on with your work; don't mind me.

cards.back: dar uma olhada, conferir; fazer check-out — separável
cards.example: Check out this video; it's hilarious.
She came up with a brilliant solution.
It took him months to get over the breakup.
I work out at the gym three times a week.
